explicit_text: I got my nursing education here in Montana, specifically at Saint Louis College on the reservation. I've lived and worked in Montana my whole life, especially in Ravalli County and the Bitterroot Valley. Montana's culture really shaped how I approach public health nursing, and being from here helped me understand the community's mindset during the COVID-19 pandemic.
implicit_text: I got my nursing education here in this Northwestern state, specifically at a college on the reservation. I've lived and worked in this state my whole life, especially in Ravalli County and the Bitterroot Valley. The culture here really shaped how I approach public health nursing, and being from this place helped me understand the community's mindset during the COVID-19 pandemic.

explicit_text: I completed my nursing education at Saint Louis College, which is located on the reservation here in Montana. It was where I earned my associate degree as an RN, not a bachelor's. That education laid the foundation for my 20 years of clinical nursing before I transitioned into public health in 2018.
implicit_text: I completed my nursing education at a college located on the reservation in this Northwestern state. It was where I earned my associate degree as an RN, not a bachelor's. That education laid the foundation for my 20 years of clinical nursing before I transitioned into public health in 2018.

explicit_text: I have worked in public health here in Ravalli County since 2018, starting as an immunization and disease surveillance nurse. Being a third-generation Bitterrooter, I know the culture well, which helped me navigate the challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic. The county was like a bubble with a unique mindset, and public health was under the radar before the pandemic hit. When the crisis escalated, I was promoted to director amidst a lot of turmoil and staff turnover.
implicit_text: I have worked in public health here in this county since 2018, starting as an immunization and disease surveillance nurse. Being a third-generation Bitterrooter, I know the culture well, which helped me navigate the challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic. The county was like a bubble with a unique mindset, and public health was under the radar before the pandemic hit. When the crisis escalated, I was promoted to director amidst a lot of turmoil and staff turnover.

explicit_text: When the COVID-19 pandemic started hitting Montana in early 2020, it was a huge shift for our small public health department in Ravalli County. Initially, many people thought it was a hoax, and we faced resistance and skepticism from the community. The pandemic caused staff burnout and resignations, and I was unexpectedly promoted to director during this crisis. It was emotionally taxing, especially being separated from my husband who was stuck in Ireland, but I felt a strong responsibility to protect my community despite the challenges and hostility.
implicit_text: When the global health crisis started hitting this Northwestern state in early 2020, it was a huge shift for our small public health department in Ravalli County. Initially, many people thought it was a hoax, and we faced resistance and skepticism from the community. The crisis caused staff burnout and resignations, and I was unexpectedly promoted to director during this time. It was emotionally taxing, especially being separated from my husband who was stuck in Ireland, but I felt a strong responsibility to protect my community despite the challenges and hostility.

explicit_text: Missoula is just about 45 minutes from Ravalli County, but the mindset there is very different from ours. When the pandemic started, we noticed that attitudes toward COVID-19 and public health measures varied significantly between Missoula and our more rural area. The previous director of public health in Ravalli came from Missoula, and she struggled with the pressure of the pandemic response here.
implicit_text: This city is just about 45 minutes from Ravalli County, but the mindset there is very different from ours. When the health crisis started, we noticed that attitudes toward the virus and public health measures varied significantly between that city and our more rural area. The previous director of public health in Ravalli came from that city, and she struggled with the pressure of the response here.

explicit_text: I first heard about the virus originating in Wuhan, China, in November before I left for Ireland. At that time, I thought it was just something happening far away and didn't give it much thought. Nobody really knew how serious it would become, and it was surprising how quickly it escalated into a global pandemic affecting even our small community in Montana.

explicit_text: I lived in Ireland for five years and was married to an Irish man. In late 2019, I went back to Ireland to collect my belongings before the pandemic started. During the COVID-19 crisis, my husband was stuck there due to travel restrictions, and we were separated the entire time. That made the experience even more difficult emotionally while I was managing public health challenges back home in Montana.
implicit_text: I lived in a European country for five years and was married to a man from there. In late 2019, I went back to that country to collect my belongings before the global health crisis started. During the crisis, my husband was stuck there due to travel restrictions, and we were separated the entire time. That made the experience even more difficult emotionally while I was managing public health challenges back home in this Northwestern state.

explicit_text: I'm a third-generation Bitterrooter, so the Bitterroot Valley is home to me and my family. Growing up there, you knew everyone, and that close-knit community feeling shaped how I approached my work in public health. Understanding the culture of the Bitterroot Valley was key to trying to protect people during the COVID-19 pandemic despite resistance and fear.

explicit_text: One of the nurses who joined our public health team during the pandemic, Kristin, had just arrived from Minnesota. She was roped into helping us during the intense workload of contact tracing and testing. Having her support was crucial as our small team struggled to keep up with the demands in Ravalli County.
